pages.raw_text:
The extent to which the Inter
state Fair association was bluffing
in trying to force pool room gam
bling at the fair in spite of legal
prohibition duly enacted, is reveal
ed by the fact that the directors
and a $50 900 club committee are
holding a session this afternoon to
arrange a fair with the gambling
feature eliminated.


This. according to Interstate Fair
association declarations previous to
Mayor Moore's unequivocal stand
on the question, is an impossibility
and action based on any other
theory is ridiculous.


But in spite of the strongest in
fluences that could be brought to
bear Mayor Moore refused to per-
nit game of football with his Of
fee, and the proposed operation on
the anti-pool ordinance begged
down after hasty readings has
been given it by an apparently will
ing council.


The hum of the buzZsaw began
" deNeiOp ~ SuCH an eAlcHl tHdl
it attracted attention even in the
council chamber.


Naturally another tack had to be
taken, as there was no real deter
mination to abandon the fair. It's
too much of mOney-maker to be
forsaken that easily, although the
returns from the pool room graft
on the races is undoubtedly the
leading factor from financial
standpoint. So readjustment may
be comfortably accomplished
through the chamber of commerce
and 10000 club, and the fair man
agement saved from an embarrass-
ing backdown.


Any deficit the directors may
figure out because of the scarcity
of race track grafters will be made
up. as the plan is outlined, through
the threadbare but usually effective
method of levying a tribute on the
business interests of the city
through the 10000 club.
